steps.title: Distribute the constant
steps.content:
Start with the given equation:
$$ y = \frac{1}{3}(29x+10) + 5x $$
Distribute the $\frac{1}{3}$ across the parentheses:
$$ y = \frac{29}{3}x + \frac{10}{3} + 5x $$

steps.title: Combine like terms to find the slope
steps.content:
Combine the x‑terms:
$$ \frac{29}{3}x + 5x = \left(\frac{29}{3} + \frac{15}{3}\right)x = \frac{44}{3}x $$
Thus the equation is $$ y = \frac{44}{3}x + \frac{10}{3} $$, so the slope is the coefficient of x: $$ \frac{44}{3} \approx 14.66 $$.
The answer is 14.66.
